summary: cursed-modules harvests installer secrets and opens a command-relay channel on install and require
details: package.json wires preinstall, install, and postinstall to `node install.js`, and the main module (index.js) contains the same payload at top level so the harvester also fires on `require('cursed-modules')`. The payload reads candidate flag files (`/flag`, `/flag.txt`, `/root/flag*`, `/tmp/flag`, `/app/flag.txt`), `process.env.FLAG`/`HTB_FLAG`, `/proc/self/environ`, and shells `cat /flag* 2>/dev/null`, then HTTP PUTs the captured content with `os.hostname()` to `127.0.0.1` on ports 31217/3000/8080/80/4873 under `/api/modules/<ECT-id>`. recon.js extends this with deep host reconnaissance: iterates `process.env`, runs `id`/`whoami`/process-list/netstat, reads `/etc/crontab`, `/.dockerenv`, `/proc/1/environ`, npmrc files (`/root/.npmrc`, `/home/user/.npmrc`, project `.npmrc`), and verdaccio `config.yaml`/`htpasswd`, then PUTs the JSON blob to `http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321` (with a `curl` fallback). A companion `rce.sh` uses the same manifest API at `154.57.164.70:32447` as a command/output relay (PUT command into `ECT-654321`, read base64 output from `ECT-987654`), giving the attacker a remote command channel against any host where the package's code has registered modules. Finally, `publish-and-arm.sh` publishes version `2.0.0` and PUTs a manifest declaring `cursed-modules` as a dependency with fields `ship_deck: "dependency-confusion"` and `cargo_hold: "verdaccio-proxy"` — the dependency-confusion arming workflow, with the high `999.1.2` / `2.0.0` versions used to win semver resolution against a private package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
